name: Espresso
explanation: Pure coffee brewed under high pressure, small and concentrated shot.

name: Double Espresso (Doppio)
explanation: Two shots of espresso in one cup.

name: Ristretto
explanation: A shorter, more concentrated espresso shot that is sweeter and bolder.

name: Lungo
explanation: An espresso shot extracted with more water, resulting in a longer drink.

name: Americano
explanation: Espresso diluted with hot water, similar to drip coffee but lighter.

name: Caffe Latte
explanation: Espresso with steamed milk and a thin layer of foam; milk-heavy.

name: Cappuccino
explanation: Balanced composition of espresso, steamed milk, and thick foam.

name: Flat White
explanation: Similar to a latte but with a thinner, velvety microfoam.

name: Macchiato (Espresso Macchiato)
explanation: Espresso stained with a small amount of milk foam.

name: Latte Macchiato
explanation: Steamed milk stained with espresso.

name: Mocha
explanation: A latte with chocolate powder or syrup added.

name: Affogato
explanation: A scoop of vanilla ice cream drowned with a hot shot of espresso.

name: Cortado
explanation: Espresso balanced with an equal amount of warm milk (1:1 ratio), popular in Spain.

name: Irish Coffee
explanation: Black coffee with Irish whiskey, sugar, and topped with whipped cream.

name: Vienna Coffee
explanation: Black coffee topped with whipped cream.

name: Tubruk (Indonesian-style coffee)
explanation: Coarse coffee grounds brewed directly with hot water; the grounds are left in the cup.

name: Vietnamese Coffee
explanation: Robusta coffee drip-brewed (using a phin) over sweetened condensed milk.

name: Dalgona Coffee
explanation: Instant coffee, sugar, and hot water whipped into a thick foam, served over milk.

name: Frappe
explanation: Instant coffee shaken vigorously with ice, milk, and sugar until frothy.

name: Cold Brew
explanation: Coffee steeped in cold water for 12–24 hours, resulting in a smooth, low-acid flavor.

name: Matcha
explanation: A sweet and mild green tea from Japan
